chapter_title.en: Chapter on Divorce According to the Sunnah

text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: "إِذَا طَلَّقَ اَلرَّجُلُ اِمْرَأَتَهُ وَ أَشْهَدَ شَاهِدَيْنِ عَدْلَيْنِ فِي قُبُلِ عِدَّتِهَا فَلَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يُطَلِّقَهَا بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى تَنْقَضِيَ عِدَّتُهَا أَوْ يُرَاجِعَهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4755 - Bukayr ibn Aʿyan narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"If a man divorces his wife and brings two just witnesses to bear witness to it during her waiting period ('iddah), he cannot divorce her again after that until her waiting period ends or he reconciles with her."
